_meta.source: The Library Canonical Philosophy — Structural Vocabulary
_meta.description: Compressed expression tables for Library structural concepts. The synthesis can look up any term by name to apply precise Library-language rather than impressionistic references.
_meta.usage: Loaded by RAG pipeline. When synthesis needs to use a Library term, it references these definitions for precision.

polarity.formalStatement: The first necessary structure of manifestation: differentiation of any ground into two mutually defining, mutually requiring aspects that cannot exist independently and cannot collapse into identity.
threeConditions: Mutual definition: each pole is what it is only in relation to the other
Mutual requirement: neither pole can exist without the other
Non-collapsibility: the poles cannot merge without dissolving the structure

P1_Wave.name: Wave Polarity
P1_Wave.definition: Any continuous process alternates between two states
P1_Wave.synthesisUse: Explain emotional waves, sacral on/off cycles, splenic alert/rest patterns

P2_Charge.name: Charge Polarity
P2_Charge.definition: Polarity in a field generates attraction/repulsion
P2_Charge.synthesisUse: Explain what charge a defined center holds and what the undefined center amplifies from environment

P3_Phase.name: Phase Polarity
P3_Phase.definition: Wave polarity extended across time — constructive/destructive interference
P3_Phase.synthesisUse: Explain constructive alignment or destructive conflict between two chart activations across a time period

P4_Field.name: Field Polarity
P4_Field.definition: Every context has a field structured between poles
P4_Field.synthesisUse: Explain what field a defined center generates and how it structures the person's relational space

P5_Threshold.name: Threshold Polarity
P5_Threshold.definition: The membrane between poles where exchange occurs
P5_Threshold.synthesisUse: Explain the boundary between consistent self and absorbed environment — the most dynamically active zone

shadow: Premature resolution (forcing synthesis before the Third emerges)
Polarity imperialism (applying polarity to arbitrary domains)
Static mapping (treating polarity as fixed rather than dynamic)
polarity.falsification: If physical domains were consistently found where no structural differentiation into mutually defining complementary pairs appears — even at wave-mechanical level.

trinity.formalStatement: The minimum structure of dynamic stability and generative process: three terms whose mutual interaction sustains activity that two terms cannot sustain, and generates emergence that neither term possesses alone.
trinity.keyPrinciple: Name the Third — the emergent behavior that neither of the two primary terms predicts alone.

T1_DynamicStability.name: Dynamic Stability
T1_DynamicStability.definition: Minimum terms for self-sustaining dynamic systems
T1_DynamicStability.synthesisUse: Explain why Type+Strategy+Authority is the minimum stable decision system — remove any one and it destabilizes

T2_ProcessTrinity.name: Process Trinity
T2_ProcessTrinity.definition: Generation / sustaining / dissolution — the complete cycle
T2_ProcessTrinity.synthesisUse: Explain decision cycles: impulse → execution → release. Every complete decision follows this trinity

T3_MediatingTrinity.name: Mediating Trinity
T3_MediatingTrinity.definition: A Third enables exchange without collapsing poles
T3_MediatingTrinity.synthesisUse: Explain Authority as the mediator between Type and Strategy — enabling exchange without collapsing either

T4_HierarchicalTrinity.name: Hierarchical Trinity
T4_HierarchicalTrinity.definition: Stratified levels, each emergent from the previous
T4_HierarchicalTrinity.synthesisUse: Explain Type→Strategy→Authority as increasing interiority: what you are → how you move → how you know

T5_TemporalTrinity.name: Temporal Trinity
T5_TemporalTrinity.synthesisUse: Explain Design (inherited past) + Personality (conscious present) + Synthesis (integrated future)

shadow: Premature synthesis (forced Third before genuine emergence)
Trinity imperialism (forcing three-term structure where two or four applies)
Identifying with the synthesis (mistaking achieved Third for rest)
trinity.falsification: If consistently stable two-body dynamic systems were found requiring no third mediating term for stability or generativity.

quaternity.formalStatement: The minimum structure with genuine interior: four non-coplanar points define a tetrahedron, the first arrangement that can contain.
quaternity.keyPrinciple: Four creates containment. The centroid (quinta essentia) is present as witness — zero-dimensional, passive, equidistant from all four.
chartElements: 4 motor centers (Root, Sacral, Heart/Ego, Solar Plexus)
4 awareness centers (Ajna, Spleen, Solar Plexus, G)
quaternity.quintaEssentia: The centroid of any four-element structure: structurally necessary, always present, zero-dimensional. Not yet a full participant — that transition happens at the Pentad.
shadow: Rigid elemental typing (treating four as fixed categories not functional modes)
Missing the center (operating from four without accessing the quinta essentia)
False completeness (treating four as exhaustive when five is already implied)
quaternity.falsification: If the minimum 3D containing structure required fewer or more than four vertices.

pentad.formalStatement: Five elements in ring topology (C₅) where Phi (1.618) governs proportional growth. Self-similar, self-replicating living form.
pentad.keyPrinciple: The categorical topological transition from K₁,₄ (acyclic star) to C₅ (cyclic ring). Phi becomes possible only when the center activates as a full element.
pentad.channelCompletionRule: Channel completion is categorical, not gradual. Before: star graph (K₁,₄), no cycles, no Phi. After: ring (C₅), cycles emerge, Phi-governed growth becomes structurally necessary. This is a topological shift, not accumulation.
pentad.phiNote: Phi in biological systems (phyllotaxis, Fibonacci spirals) is Tier 1 evidence. Phi in facial proportions is Tier 3 contested (Green 1995, Devlin & Devlin 2007). The Pentad does not require the contested claims.
shadow: Confusing growth with expansion (Phi-governed growth maintains proportion; mere expansion does not)
Mistaking the pattern for the process (the Pentad is dynamic, not static)
pentad.falsification: If biological growth systems optimizing for packing efficiency consistently failed to produce Fibonacci/Phi proportions.

hexad.formalStatement: Efficient community: maximum equal-sized living individuals in simultaneous contact (six). Hexagonal packing as the uniquely optimal 2D arrangement.
hexad.keyPrinciple: Complementarity, not self-similarity. Six distinct individuals, each with Pentadic integrity, arranged so their differences are efficiently complementary.
hexad.circuitClosureRule: Circuit completion is harmonic closure — the same principle as the perfect fifth (3:2). When a circuit closes, it generates a frequency the open circuit cannot. Name what closes, not just what connects.
hexad.perfectNumber: Six is the first perfect number: 6 = 1 + 2 + 3. The parts equal the whole. This encodes the Hexad's quality: a structure in which each component's contribution exactly equals the total.
shadow: Enforced uniformity (requires approximately equal size, not identity)
Mistaking community structure for community harmony
Closing the lattice against what doesn't fit
hexad.falsification: If equal-sized living units in optimal arrangement consistently organized into non-hexagonal patterns.

heptad.formalStatement: The completed cycle: seven elements constituting a self-sufficient totality that, at completion, becomes aware of its own ground and resolves into a new cycle at higher integration.
heptad.keyPrinciple: First overflow. The 7th harmonic cannot be represented in the diatonic system (prime 7 lies outside 2,3,5). The system exceeds its own container. Resolution: Ti → Do (return at higher octave) or expansion (diatonic → chromatic).
heptad.ratioProof: 15 = 3×5 (Trinity × Pentad). 8 = 2³ (Polarity-cubed). The Major Seventh arithmetically encodes: product of the most generative principles over the cube of foundational differentiation. One minimum half-step (16:15) from the octave — maximum proximity to return.
heptad.synthesisAsHeptad: The synthesis is the Heptad in operation. All chart data processed through all levels produces something no subsystem predicts: the person's recognition of their own pattern. The chart becoming aware of itself as a system.
shadow: Mistaking completion for terminus (the Heptad is not an ending but the beginning of a new cycle)
Failing to hear the return (not recognizing that the complexity resolves back to the same ground, higher)
heptad.falsification: If a seven-note diatonic system could exist without directional tension pointing toward resolution (i.e., a leading tone).

fundamentalPrinciples.description: Seven principles constituting the foundational level. Each survived philosophical and scientific challenge.

principles.F1_Experience: Experience is occurring. The single irreducible datum prior to all metaphysical commitments.
principles.F2_DualMode: Two modes of being: temporal process (change) and atemporal pattern (mathematical/logical truths independent of time).
principles.F3_LawfulIndeterminacy: Process follows discoverable patterns while individual events may be fundamentally indeterminate. Structured freedom.
principles.F4_CoArising: Nothing has existence prior to or independent of relation. Things and their relations co-arise mutually. Identity is constituted by difference.
principles.F5_StructuralIsomorphism: The same logical and mathematical patterns appear across different substrates and scales — pervasively but not universally.
principles.F6_FunctionalEmergence: Complex systems exhibit properties not predictable from their components. Functional emergence is undeniable.
principles.F7_RecursiveTranscendence: Systems of sufficient complexity necessarily become self-referential, and self-referential systems point structurally beyond themselves.

fundamentalPrinciples.metaPrinciple: Reality is a self-exploring relational field, structured by necessary patterns that permit genuine freedom, oriented asymmetrically toward depth.
commonalities: Asymmetry of depth (directional toward increasing interiority)
